props.message:
Here three peoples agreed not to fight.

The agreement is older than any of them remember; none can name what it was made over.

root.speaker: Sela
root.text: Wanderer. If you've coin, head to the alleys yonder. If you've ears, mind which side of the plaza you bend them to.

trade.speaker: Sela
trade.text: Iron, salt, sweetwood, knot-rope. Whatever moves at a fair price. We don't sell air — leave that to the poets across the way.

sharers.speaker: Sela
sharers.text: Insufferable, in the politest terms. They say they're the soul of the Sprawl. The soul does not weigh out grain at dusk. We do.

locals.speaker: Sela
locals.text: Honest folk. Suspicious folk. They keep their gate closed and their porch swept. I respect that, even when they snub our scales.

bye.speaker: Sela
bye.text: Mind your purse. The plaza's quieter than it looks.

root.speaker: Vahn
root.text: Walk well, walker. You stand on the only patch of ground in the Sprawl that all three peoples agree is no one's. Beautiful, isn't it.

sharers.speaker: Vahn
sharers.text: We tend the verses, the plinths, the bell. The Binds say we sell nothing. We give. They cannot tell the difference — which, I suppose, is the difference.

verse.speaker: Vahn
verse.text:
*“Three roads, one breath.
The stones forget their cutters,
the bell forgets its hour,
but the air remembers we stood here.”*

thanks.speaker: Vahn
thanks.text: Lirien's breath. Few say so without a smirk these days.

plaza.speaker: Vahn
plaza.text: Older than any of us, the agreement. The pillar there is older than the agreement. The stones beneath the pillar — older yet. We are the youngest things here.

bye.speaker: Vahn
bye.text: Walk well, and listen as you go.

pass.speaker: Garth
pass.text: Good. Pass through, then. ...You're not armed like a cutpurse. That's something.

frontier.speaker: Garth
frontier.text: Hard wood. Hard wind. The kind of quiet that makes a man hear his own thoughts and not like them. We build, we mend, we keep watch. It suits us.

standoff.speaker: Garth
standoff.text: Couldn't tell you. The Binds reckon coin. The Sharers reckon verses. We reckon neither and neither reckons us. The pillar reckons all three, and says nothing.
